lede: A look at September 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Talitha Bateman and Xavier Woods, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2024 Apalachee High School shooting
events.desc: A 14-year-old gunman kills four people and injures seven in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School near Winder, Georgia.

events.title: Al-Qaeda
events.desc: Three terrorists suspected to be a part of Al-Qaeda are arrested in Germany after allegedly planning attacks on both the Frankfurt International airport and US military installations.

events.title: Leipzig
events.desc: In Leipzig, East Germany, the first of weekly demonstration for the legalisation of opposition groups and democratic reforms takes place.

events.title: Golden Dragon massacre
events.desc: The Golden Dragon massacre takes place in San Francisco.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: Operation Swift begins when U.S. Marines engage the North Vietnamese in battle in the Que Son Valley.

events.title: Wilhelmina of the Netherlands
events.desc: Queen Wilhelmina of the Netherlands abdicates for health reasons.

events.title: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk
events.desc: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, who founded the Republic of Turkey, gathers a congress in Sivas to make decisions as to the future of Anatolia and Thrace.

events.title: Battle of Kowloon
events.desc: Battle of Kowloon: British vessels open fire on Chinese war junks enforcing a food sales embargo on the British community in China in the first armed conflict of the First Opium War.

events.title: Great Fire of London
events.desc: In London, England, the most destructive damage from the Great Fire occurs.

events.title: Flight of the Earls
events.desc: The Flight of the Earls takes place in Ireland (14 September N.S.).

births.name: Marita Ulvskog
births.line: Swedish politician, Deputy Prime Minister of Sweden

births.name: Albert Orsborn
births.line: English 6th General of The Salvation Army (died 1967)

deaths.text: César-François Cassini de Thury, French astronomer and cartographer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Catherine of Racconigi
Christian feast day: Moses and Aaron (Lutheran Church and Eastern Orthodox Church)
